intro: Stereochilus dalatensis is a species of orchid native to Vietnam, commonly known as the Dalat Stereochilus. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, pain, and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Stereochilus dalatensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Vietnamese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to treat various health issues, and its extracts have also been used in the development of modern herbal supplements and pharmaceuticals.
